task_completion.description: Vital Recorder is open with the 0003.vital file loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder application window is visible, and the title bar or interface indicates that '0003.vital' is the active file.

task_completion.description: The timeline is navigated to the 'Surgery started' event.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An event marker, vertical line, or text indicating 'Surgery started' is visible in the timeline or event list, and the waveform view is centered or positioned around this event.

task_completion.description: The timeline zoom is adjusted to display approximately 10-30 minutes of data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The time axis (usually at the bottom or top of the waveform tracks) shows a total visible time span of roughly 10 to 30 minutes from the left edge to the right edge of the screen.

task_completion.description: Individual waveform patterns are clearly visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The waveform tracks display distinct, repeating spikes or waves corresponding to individual heartbeats in the ECG and arterial pressure/PLETH pulsations, rather than being compressed into a solid block of color.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Vital Recorder GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the genuine Vital Recorder interface with its standard menus, toolbars, and track displays, rather than a static image viewer or text editor.
The application shows '0003.vital' and the expected tracks (ECG, PLETH, etc.) rather than a different dataset.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Vital Recorder 1.16.6 should be used. The file '0003.vital' contains 1h 13m 14s of data with tracks including ECG_II, ECG_V5, PLETH, and various airway pressures. The file contains standard event markers including 'Surgery started'. The VLM should verify that the time axis shows a 10-30 minute span and that the 'Surgery started' marker is in view.
